meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 8)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Mixed pronoun exercises with animals, classmates, and story sentences.

questions.question: Which pronoun replaces a group of people?
questions.explanation: "they" is used to replace a group of people or things.

questions.question: "i love dogs" is correctly written.
questions.explanation: The pronoun 'I' must always be capitalized. Correct: "I love dogs."

questions.question: Mina sees a cute camel. → ___ sees a cute camel.
questions.explanation: Mina is female → use 'She'.

questions.question: "Me" is used when you are the subject of the sentence.
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject. "Me" is the object (comes after verbs/prepositions).

questions.question: Which sentence is correct? "___ have so much fun in the desert."
questions.explanation: "I have" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'have'.

questions.question: The children are playing outside. → ___ are playing outside. (one word)
questions.explanation: "the children" is plural → use 'They'.

questions.question: The cat likes to chase the mouse. → ___ likes to chase the mouse.
questions.explanation: "The cat" is an animal (gender not specified) → use 'It'.

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: I/she/it = singular. they/we = plural.

questions.question: In the sentence "It gives me an idea," the pronoun 'me' is used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'gives' (as the object). This is correct.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ am surprised! (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'I' is the subject — it comes before the verb. I am surprised!

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I have so much fun in the desert." 'I' is the subject.

questions.question: Which sentence uses pronouns correctly?
questions.explanation: "I live in the ranch." — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'live'.

questions.question: In the sentence "Mommy and I put our clothes in it," 'I' is used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Mommy and I' is the subject of the sentence. 'I' is correctly used before the verb.

questions.question: The boys are playing soccer. → ___ are playing soccer.
questions.explanation: "The boys" is plural → use 'They'.

questions.question: Fill in: Can you give ___ the pencil? (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'me' is the object — it comes after the verb. Can you give me the pencil?

questions.question: Match each noun phrase with the correct pronoun:
questions.explanation: It = thing/animal. They = plural. She = female. He = male.

questions.question: Which sentence has a WRONG pronoun?
options: I am not scared of the lion.
questions.explanation: "roars at I" is wrong — after a preposition, use 'me': "The lion roars at me."

questions.question: The sentence "She always helps me with homework" is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'helps' (object). This is correct.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Please give me the book." 'me' is the object after 'give'.

questions.question: Which sentence has the correct pronoun to replace the underlined noun? "The apples are yummy!"
questions.explanation: "The apples" is plural → use 'They'. "They are yummy!"
